channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Rethinking Sexuality in Prehistory: Cooperation, Social Bonds, and Evolutionary Strategy
item.description: This article challenges the stereotypical image of prehistoric humans as brutish, violence-driven beings who engaged in purely force-based relationships. Instead, it argues that sexual behavior in pre...

item.title: Federal government pressed to model emissions impact of recent policy changes
item.description: The federal government of Canada is facing mounting pressure from opposition MPs, experts, and environmental groups to clearly demonstrate how recent policy changes are affecting the country’s greenho...

item.title: Humility and True Influence: What Bollywood Icons Teach Us About Power
item.description: The article explores the relationship between celebrity, ego, and true power through anecdotes involving Bollywood legends and prominent industrialists. It contrasts the behaviour of stars like Kareen...

item.title: Abhishek Banerjee allegedly manhandled during visit to Sonarpur amid post-poll violence tensions
item.description: Trinamool Congress (TMC) national general secretary and MP Abhishek Banerjee was allegedly attacked and manhandled during his visit to Sonarpur in South 24 Parganas, West Bengal, on Saturday. He had g...

item.title: Weekly Superquiz and Mini Crossword Feature for Sunday, May 31
item.description: The article promotes the Sunday edition of the Superquiz and associated interactive puzzles published by The Sydney Morning Herald. It highlights the availability of the Superquiz for readers on Sunda...

item.title: Tanzania calls for stronger gender equality in higher education leadership
item.description: Tanzania’s ambassador to France, Ambassador Saidi Othman Yakubu, has called on higher learning institutions to strengthen efforts towards achieving gender equality, especially in leadership positions ...

item.title: DOST retires over 3,300 worn Philippine flags ahead of Independence Day, reminds public of proper disposal law
item.description: The Department of Science and Technology (DOST), through the Philippine Textile Research Institute (DOST-PTRI), has properly retired more than 3,300 worn Philippine flags ahead of the country’s Indepe...

item.title: Christopher Operi replaces injured Clement Akpa in Ivory Coast World Cup squad
item.description: Ivory Coast have had to make a last-minute adjustment to their 2026 World Cup squad after defender Clement Akpa was sidelined due to an adductor injury sustained during training with his French club, ...

item.title: Andreas Feininger’s 1947 Photograph of a Texaco Station on Route 66 in Seligman, Arizona
item.description: The article discusses a renowned 1947 photograph taken by Andreas Feininger in Seligman, Arizona, depicting a Texaco gas station along the iconic Route 66. The image is celebrated for its striking com...

item.title: Rocket explosion damages Blue Origin launch pad, leading to months-long delay in operations
item.description: Blue Origin is expected to face significant operational delays after a rocket explosion during a test fire severely damaged its launch pad. According to company and industry sources, the incident has ...

item.title: Five coastal towns for a perfect winter escape in South Africa
item.description: South Africa offers a refreshing winter escape with its coastline, where quieter shores, dramatic seascapes, and whale watching replace the usual summer crowds. This piece highlights five coastal town...

item.title: Zanzibar to roll out standardised taxi meter and digital payment system to improve transport transparency
item.description: Zanzibar is planning to introduce a modern taxi metering and digital payment system aimed at improving transparency, service quality, and efficiency within the islands’ transport sector. The initiativ...

item.title: Turaki-led PDP relocates convention venue and endorses Goodluck Jonathan for 2027 election after police blockade
item.description: The Turaki-led faction of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) in Nigeria has reportedly changed the venue of its convention in Abuja after police officers blocked access roads leading to the original l...

item.title: Debate over Bill C-9 raises concerns about free expression and hate speech laws in Canada
item.description: The article by Conrad Black examines the controversy surrounding Bill C-9, also known as the Combating Hate Act, which has passed the House of Commons and is now under scrutiny for its broad definitio...

item.title: Concerns Over Nike’s Recycled World Cup Uniforms and the Limits of Chemical Recycling
item.description: Nike has introduced a new initiative ahead of the World Cup in which uniforms for 16 national teams are made using recycled materials derived from textile waste. The company claims the jerseys were pr...

item.title: Federal Judge Temporarily Blocks Trump Administration’s Anti-Weaponization Fund
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked the Trump administration’s $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund, established as part of a settlement involving former President Trump, his family, and the Tru...

item.title: Karachi hospitals report increase in stomach illness cases during Eid ul Adha due to heavy meat consumption
item.description: During the Eid ul Adha holidays, public hospitals in Karachi reported a noticeable increase in patients suffering from stomach-related problems. According to medical staff, emergency departments remai...

item.title: AUKUS partners launch joint underwater drone programme to protect seabed infrastructure
item.description: The United States, the United Kingdom and Australia have announced a joint initiative to develop advanced underwater drone technology under their AUKUS defence partnership. The uncrewed undersea vehic...

item.title: Forensic archaeology reveals burial sites and legacy of the Namibian genocide
item.description: This article explores how forensic archaeology is shedding new light on the Namibian genocide that took place between 1904 and 1908 under German colonial rule, during which tens of thousands of Ovaher...

item.title: Paris Saint-Germain and Arsenal Prepare for Champions League Final in Budapest
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain and Arsenal are set to meet in the 2026 UEFA Champions League final at Budapest's Puskás Aréna. PSG, the defending champions, aim to become only the second team after Real Madrid t...
